title: Rice pudding – just for the fun of it
excerpt: Rice pudding is often seen as the Cinderella of puddings. If you dress her up in a pretty little frock, you can let her sit at your table. YOU NEED; 330 ml rice – I used Spekko's Jasmine rice 30 ml butter 2 tins coconut cream 125 ml sugar …

seo.title: Rice pudding - just for the fun of it - Marinda Kook

html:
Rice pudding is often seen as the Cinderella of puddings. If you dress her up in a pretty little frock, you can let her sit at your table.

 

YOU NEED;

  • 330 ml rice – I used Spekko's Jasmine rice
  • 30 ml butter
  • 2 tins coconut cream
  • 125 ml sugar
  • 1 vanilla pod or 5 ml vanilla extract
  • 1 cinnamon stick
  • 3 cardamom seeds, crushed
  • pinch of salt
  • slab of chocolate of your choice – can be milk chocolate, dark, light or mixed

NOW

  1. melt the butter in a saucepan and stir-fry the rice until all the grains are glossy
  2. add all the rest of the ingredients except the chocolate
  3. now you have to stir continuously – it can easily catch and burn
  4. after 20 minutes it should have a creamy texture. Taste it. The grains shouldn't be mushy.
  5. take out the vanilla pod, cardamom and cinnamon sticks and throw them away
  6. take it off the stove and stir in the chocolate

SYRUP

  1. in a microwave-safe container put 125 g berries (any kind) 100 ml sugar and 100ml water.
  2. microwave for about 5 minutes – stir and mash the berries a little

Now spoon your rice pudding into a nice dish and spoon the berries on top. It's cheap, easy and deeeeeeeelicious.
